danger_signals.visual_form: Soda cans and candy wrappers rendered on basketball court floor
danger_signals.meaning: Tripping hazard; ball handler will fall and lose possession if stepped on

danger_signals.visual_form: Referee positioned on court as visual obstacle
danger_signals.meaning: Player can fall over referee, resulting in ball loss to opponent

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual proximity and animation of opponent character
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent threat of being punched or having ball stolen via flying leap maneuver

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent character jumping forward at ball carrier with tackling motion
danger_signals.meaning: High-risk ball theft attempt; successful execution results in knockdown

safety_signals.visual_form: Ball held visually by player character sprite
safety_signals.meaning: Control state; player can score or pass

safety_signals.visual_form: Second team character positioned on court near ball carrier
safety_signals.meaning: Available passing option to reset play or create scoring opportunity

safety_signals.visual_form: Absence of hazard objects or opponents in immediate movement path
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone for ball handler movement toward basket

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual separation between player character and opposing team sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced immediate threat of physical interference or ball theft

faction_indicators.color_identity: Team-specific color palette (customizable; Chicago, Los Angeles, New York, Denver, Natural High, Brawl State each have distinct hues)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Team name displayed on scoreboard and player uniforms
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Away team rendered in contrasting color scheme to distinguish from home team
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Team colors reflected in uniform sprites; scoreboard displays home team on left side

faction_indicators.color_identity: Contrasting color palette to home team (operator-customizable)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Team name displayed on scoreboard and player uniforms
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct sprite coloring and uniform appearance from home team
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Team colors reflected in uniform sprites; scoreboard displays away team on right side

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ball visible on court; both player characters animated and responsive; scoreboard displays current score and time
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players move freely, pass, shoot, and punch opponents without referee penalty
Possession transfers to opposing team; referee enforces shot clock rule (only penalty called in game)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scoreboard indicates overtime period; court may show visual tension cue
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single-point sudden death scoring; next basket wins; overtime period lasts one minute before repeat if no score

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Soda cans or candy wrappers rendered on floor at specific locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ball carrier stumbles and falls if stepping on object; opponent gains ball theft opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player character sprite transitions to fallen/horizontal animation; ball visible on court floor
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Possession becomes vulnerable; opponent can freely steal ball without penalty

environmental_storytelling.example: Litter (soda cans, candy wrappers) on floor creates uncontrolled play zones; implies casual/rowdy court environment matching 'Basket Brawl' tagline
Referee positioned on court as both authority figure and physical hazard; reinforces that contact is permitted but chaos remains manageable
Distinct uniform colors and player sprite variations between home/away teams create immediate faction recognition and rivalry visual narrative
Eight playable characters each with unique characteristics (documented but not detailed in source); implies player identity and playstyle differentiation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can pass to teammate, shoot at basket, or punch opposing player without penalty; animation shows holding/dribbling motion
Trips and falls if stepping on soda can or candy wrapper; losing possession
Can be punched freely; opponent can execute flying leap tackle to knock down and steal ball
Can execute flying leap maneuver; if successful, tackles and steals ball; if missed, rolls along floor (vulnerable state)
Rolls helplessly along floor; temporary vulnerability window before regaining control
Acts as passive obstacle; can cause ball carrier to trip and fall; enforces shot clock violations only (no physical contact penalties)
Can call for pass or direct shot; positions indicate available passing lanes

notes: Arch Rivals (Genesis 1992) is a 2v2 arcade-style basketball game emphasizing physical contact and court chaos. Visual communication is straightforward: team colors distinguish factions, hazard objects telegraph danger zones, character proximity indicates threat level, and animations clearly show possession state. The game's 'Basket Brawl' subtitle is reinforced through permissive punch mechanics and hazard-strewn court. No complex political or faction narrative exists; world-state manifestation is purely mechanical (score, time, possession, hazard presence). The Genesis port suffered from a significant gameplay balancing glitch (95% basket success rate from anywhere on court) that visually manifests as constant scoring but was not designed as intentional world-state communication.
